summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine using 2 Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with strikes hitting 11 locations and debris falling on 13 others. Russian forces reportedly breached a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone, while Ukraine struck a Russian training camp in the Nikolaev region with Geranium drones, hitting boat and unmanned craft depots. Putin stated that Russia's retaliatory strikes deep into Ukraine are far more powerful and sensitive, and that Russia will not allow Ukraine to halt the advance of Russian forces.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with 2 Oniks/Zircon missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 others (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Russian Armed Forces breached a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone, according to Putin (Source: @tass_agency/382821).
Ukraine struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Nikolaev region with Geraniums, hitting depots of boats, unmanned craft, and personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with KAB guided bombs, killing 2 and injuring 17 people including children (Source: @eRadarrua/98536).
Russia attacked Dnipropetrovsk Oblast nearly 20 times with drones and artillery, burning 3 gas stations and a sports school, injuring 2 people (Source: @UkraineNow/82233).
Ukraine is facing fuel shortages in frontline areas due to Russian strikes on gas stations using medium-range drones and Geraniums with heavier warheads (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).
Trump indicated at the G7 summit he may abandon the Anchorage agreements under which Washington agreed to Moscow's demand that Donbas remain under Russian control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31215).
Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing in 2026–2029, Finance Minister Marchenko announced at a forum in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive overnight combined-arms strike on Ukraine employing 2 Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with confirmed impacts at 11 locations and debris fallout at 13 additional locations. Russian forces reportedly breached a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone, marking a notable tactical development on the eastern front. Putin declared that Russia's retaliatory strikes deep into Ukraine are far more powerful and sensitive, signaling an escalation in Moscow's approach to long-range strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure.

points: Ukraine struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Nikolaev region using Geranium drones, hitting depots of boats and unmanned craft as well as personnel; the Russian Ministry of Defense released objective monitoring footage of the strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

Ukraine is experiencing fuel shortages in frontline areas due to Russian strikes on gas stations using medium-range drones and Geraniums with more powerful warheads, though not on the scale Kyiv typically communicates to outside observers (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).

Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029, Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko announced following meetings with UK and Lithuanian finance ministers at a forum in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).

Ukraine conducted an information-combat operation on the Crimean direction, replicating disinformation and discrediting Russia's sovereignty over Crimea, with the stated intent of gaining a stronger position in negotiations through military pressure on the peninsula (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).

A large number of drones were tracked across multiple Ukrainian oblasts: drones flew over Pokrov in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast heading north, over Novhorod-Siverskyi in Chernihiv Oblast, over Hlukhiv in Sumy Oblast, past Borynia in Sumy Oblast heading west, over Terny in Sumy Oblast, past Novodonetske in Donetsk Oblast heading northwest, toward Kropyvnytskyi in Kirovohrad Oblast, and over Novyi Buh/Kazanka in Mykolaiv Oblast heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/66654, @eRadarrua/98537, @kpszsu/66658, @kpszsu/66664, @kpszsu/66670, @kpszsu/66673, @kpszsu/66671).

Air-to-air missiles were detected over Sumy Oblast on course for Terny, and a ballistic missile threat was reported for Chuhuiv in Kharkiv Oblast and for Kharkiv city (Source: @eRadarrua/98516, @eRadarrua/98504, @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68156).

Ukrainian air defense intercepted nearly all ballistic missiles during the Kyiv attack; 5 Iskander-M were launched from Bryansk Oblast and 2 Zircon missiles from Kursk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68123).

Putin stated that Russia's retaliatory strikes deep into Ukraine are far more powerful and sensitive, and that Russia will not give Ukraine any chance to halt the advance of Russian forces (Source: @tass_agency/382814, @tass_agency/382813).
Russian Armed Forces breached a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone, according to Putin (Source: @tass_agency/382821).

Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with KAB guided bombs dropped on the regional center, killing 2 and injuring 17 people including a 5-year-old boy in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82238, @UkraineNow/82244, @eRadarrua/98536).
Russia attacked Dnipropetrovsk Oblast nearly 20 times with drones and artillery, burning 3 gas stations and a sports school, injuring 2 people (Source: @UkraineNow/82233).

Russia has gasoline reserves of 1.7 million tons, nearly corresponding to last year's levels, and previously accumulated fuel volumes have entered the domestic market; Crimea's fuel needs will be ensured with a reserve of several days (Source: @tass_agency/382796, @tass_agency/382795, @tass_agency/382812).
Putin stated that saving the Kyiv regime is not part of Russia's plans, and that the AFU wants to limit combat actions to only four oblasts to redeploy forces (Source: @tass_agency/382817).

Russia expects the arrival of American negotiators after the completion of the hot phase of events around Iran; Putin stated Russia is ready to continue discussing all modalities from Anchorage with the US (Source: @tass_agency/382827, @tass_agency/382831).
Russian Armed Forces breached a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone, according to Putin (Source: @tass_agency/382821).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Donetsk Oblast, and Kharkiv Oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66649).
A ballistic missile was reported heading toward Chuhuiv in Kharkiv Oblast, and another ballistic missile threat was issued for Kharkiv city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68137, @mon1tor_ua/68156).
A controlled drone was reported near Staryi Merchyk in Kharkiv Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/98520).
Drones were tracked past Novodonetske in Donetsk Oblast heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/66670).
Two controlled drones were reported between Synelnykove and Pavlohrad in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/98514).
Drones were tracked over Novhorod-Siverskyi in Chernihiv Oblast and heading toward Ponornytsia in northern Chernihiv Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/98537, @kpszsu/66674).
Drones moved from Sumy Oblast toward Chernihiv Oblast in the direction of Novhorod-Siverskyi (Source: @kpszsu/66659).
Drones were reported over Hlukhiv in Sumy Oblast, past Borynia heading west, and over southern Sumy Oblast heading toward Poltavshchyna (Source: @eRadarrua/98537, @kpszsu/66658, @kpszsu/66629).
A reactive drone was reported over Sumy Oblast on course for Terny, and air-to-air missiles were detected over Sumy Oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66664, @eRadarrua/98516, @eRadarrua/98504).
A reactive drone was detected near Krasnopillia in Sumy Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/98537).

A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast was reported, and a threat of Iskander-M launches from Taganrog, Rostov Oblast was also issued (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68145, @mon1tor_ua/68155).

Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with KAB guided bombs on the regional center, killing 2 and injuring 17 people, including a 5-year-old boy in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82238, @UkraineNow/82244, @eRadarrua/98536).
Ukraine struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Nikolaev region with Geraniums, hitting depots of boats, unmanned craft, and personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

Drones were tracked over Novyi Buh/Kazanka in Mykolaiv Oblast heading northwest toward Kirovohrad Oblast, and a reactive drone was detected on the border of Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk Oblasts heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/66671, @kpszsu/66688).
Drones flew over Pokrov in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast heading north, and toward Kropyvnytskyi in Kirovohrad Oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66654, @kpszsu/66673).
Russia attacked Dnipropetrovsk Oblast nearly 20 times with drones and artillery, burning 3 gas stations and a sports school, injuring 2 people (Source: @UkraineNow/82233).
A state of emergency was introduced in Crimea and Sevastopol this week to enable faster recovery efforts following Ukrainian strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).

Russia launched a massive overnight attack on Ukraine using 2 Oniks/Zircon anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones; one missile and 14 strike drones hit 11 locations, with debris falling on 13 locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Ukrainian air defense intercepted nearly all ballistic missiles during the Kyiv attack; 5 Iskander-M were launched from Bryansk Oblast and 2 Zircon missiles from Kursk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68123).

A large-scale attack on Kyiv was reported as being prepared, potentially involving many ballistic, cruise, and hypersonic missiles along with strike drones, to be launched after Tu-95MS bombers redeploy from Ukrainka airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111).
Multiple Shahed drones were reported heading toward Dnipro: 2 Shaheds directly on Dnipro and 4 Shaheds from Kharkiv Oblast to Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and onward to Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68113).

A Zircon missile was reported heading toward Kyiv, and a ballistic missile was reported toward Kyiv; there was a possibility of repeated strikes on the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68118, @mon1tor_ua/68143, @mon1tor_ua/68134).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia Oblast, Donetsk Oblast, and Kharkiv Oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66649).
Air-to-air missiles were detected over Sumy Oblast on course for Terny (Source: @eRadarrua/98516, @eRadarrua/98504).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive overnight combined-arms strike with 2 Oniks/Zircon missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, hitting 11 locations and causing debris fallout at 13 others across Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Russian Armed Forces breached a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone, according to Putin, indicating a potential tactical breakthrough on the eastern front (Source: @tass_agency/382821).
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia city center with KAB guided bombs, killing 2 and injuring 17 including a 5-year-old boy in serious condition (Source: @eRadarrua/98536, @UkraineNow/82244).
Ukraine struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces training camp in the Nikolaev region with Geranium drones, destroying depots of boats and unmanned craft along with personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31222).

Trump indicated at the G7 summit he may abandon the Anchorage agreements under which Washington agreed to Moscow's demand that Donbas remain under Russian control in any peace deal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31215).

A large-scale attack on Kyiv was reported as being prepared with ballistic, cruise, hypersonic missiles and strike drones, to follow Tu-95MS bomber redeployment from Ukrainka airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68111).

Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029, highlighting continued dependence on Western financial support despite significant international backing already provided (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is sharply escalating, with Russia conducting one of its largest combined-arms strikes of the war using Oniks/Zircon, Iskander-M/S-400, and 142 drones in a single night. The reported breach of a three-level defense line in the Center grouping zone suggests Russian forces may be achieving localized tactical breakthroughs on the eastern front. Russia's increased use of FAB guided bombs on cities like Zaporizhzhia and strikes on Ukrainian refineries and gas stations indicate Moscow has lifted some self-imposed restrictions on targeting infrastructure. The preparation of a major ballistic and hypersonic missile attack on Kyiv following Tu-95MS redeployment signals a further escalation in the air war. Ukraine's ability to strike Russian military targets, such as the Nikolaev region training camp, demonstrates continued long-range strike capability.
sections.regional_response: Trump's indication at the G7 summit that he may abandon the Anchorage agreements — under which Washington accepted Russian control of Donbas — represents a potential shift in US policy and renewed Western pressure on Russia. Putin stated Russia is ready to continue discussing Anchorage modalities with the US but expressed anger at European leaders for welcoming Ukrainian drone strikes. Finland's President Alexander Stubb assessed that the Kremlin fears domestic unrest and that growing Russian public demand for ending the war could pressure Putin toward negotiations. Ukraine's Finance Minister Marchenko secured engagement from UK and Lithuanian counterparts at the Gdansk forum, though the $146 billion financing gap for 2026–2029 underscores continued heavy reliance on Western support.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Anchorage agreements appear to be unraveling, with Trump publicly reconsidering the deal that accepted Russian control of Donbas, while Putin claims Russia was asked to make compromises formulated by American negotiators and that no signatures were placed on any document. Russia's domestic fuel situation is under strain — gasoline reserves stand at 1.7 million tons, nearly matching last year, but Ukrainian strikes on refineries have compounded the traditional summer production decline, leading to regional fuel restrictions. The AFU's information-combat operation on the Crimean direction appears designed to impress the US administration, with the assessment that only a limited amphibious operation or strike on the Crimean Bridge would be feasible, either of which could be used as a negotiation lever. The redeployment of Tu-95MS bombers from Ukrainka airfield should be monitored closely as a precursor indicator for a major strategic strike on Kyiv.
